title: Eliminar limitante con dataset grandes

Visualización Scatterplot
La visualización de tipo Scatterplot necesita 2 atributos numéricos contínuos. En este caso vamos visualizar dos variables: Miles_per_Gallon y Horsepower

scatterplot = alt.Chart(cars).mark_point().encode(
    x='Miles_per_Gallon',
    y='Horsepower'
).interactive()

scatterplot

    alt.X('life_expectancy', bin=alt.BinParams(maxbins=10)),
    alt.Y('count()')
)









Cálculo del número de bins
Obtención del número de registros n

import math

datos = gm2018['life_expectancy'].dropna().tolist()
n = len(datos)
print(f"Número de registros: {n}")

x_min = gm2018["life_expectancy"].min()
x_max = gm2018["life_expectancy"].max()

Número de registros: 178




La regla de la raíz cuadrada

k_raiz = math.ceil((math.sqrt(n)))
print(f'Regla de la raíz cuadrada: {k_raiz}')

Regla de la raíz cuadrada: 14



step = (x_max - x_min) / k_raiz

alt.Chart(gm2018).mark_bar().encode(
    alt.X(
        'life_expectancy', 
        bin=alt.BinParams(
            step=step,
            nice=False
        )
    ),
    alt.Y('count()')
)









La regla de Sturges

k_sturges = math.ceil(1 + math.log2(n))
print(f'Regla de Sturges: {k_sturges}')

Regla de Sturges: 9



step = (x_max - x_min) / k_sturges

alt.Chart(gm2018).mark_bar().encode(
    alt.X(
        'life_expectancy', 
        bin=alt.BinParams(
            step=step,
            nice=False
        )
    ),
    alt.Y('count()')
)

text:
Matriz de Pearson

corr_df = corr_df[corr_df['Variable1']<=corr_df['Variable2']]


heatmap_corr = alt.Chart(corr_df).mark_rect().encode(
    alt.X('Variable1'),
    alt.Y('Variable2'),
    alt.Color('Correlation', scale=alt.Scale(domain=[-1,1], scheme='redblue')),
    tooltip=[
        'Variable1',
        'Variable2',
        'Correlation'
    ]
).properties(
    width=300,
    height=300,
    title='Matriz de Correlación de Pearson'
)

text_corr = heatmap_corr.mark_text().encode(
    alt.Text('Correlation', format='.2f'),
    color = alt.condition(
        'abs(datum.Correlation) > 0.5',
        alt.value('white'),
        alt.value('black')
    )
)


(heatmap_corr + text_corr).configure_view(
    strokeWidth=0
)

import pandas as pd
import altair as alt
from vega_datasets import data

title: Time Series Visualization
text:
Múltiples series

highlight = alt.selection_point(
    on='pointerover',
    fields=['Major_Genre']
)

alt.Chart(movies_cleaned).mark_line(point=alt.OverlayMarkDef(
    filled=False, fill='white'
)).encode(
    alt.X('year(Release_Date)'),
    alt.Y('sum(US_Gross)'),
    alt.Color('Major_Genre'),
    opacity=alt.condition(
        highlight,
        alt.value(1),
        alt.value(0.1)
    ),
    tooltip=['year(Release_Date)','sum(US_Gross)']
).add_params(
    highlight
).interactive()

title: Time Series Visualization
text:
Múltiples series con areas

highlight = alt.selection_point(
    on='pointerover',
    fields=['Major_Genre']
)

alt.Chart(movies_cleaned).mark_area(
    line=True,
    interpolate='cardinal'
).encode(
    alt.X('year(Release_Date)'),
    alt.Y('sum(US_Gross)'),
    alt.Color('Major_Genre'),
    opacity=alt.condition(
        highlight,
        alt.value(1),
        alt.value(0.1)
    ),
    tooltip=['year(Release_Date)','sum(US_Gross)']
).add_params(
    highlight
).interactive()

Stemming y Lemmatization

from nltk.stem import PorterStemmer as stemmer
from nltk.stem import WordNetLemmatizer as lemmatizer
from nltk.corpus import wordnet

word = 'changing'

print("Stemming: ",stemmer().stem(word))
print("Lemmatization: ", lemmatizer().lemmatize(word,pos=wordnet.VERB))

title: Geo Viz
text:
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd
import altair as alt

title: Geo Viz
text:
Focus y filter

alt.Chart(gdf_ne).mark_geoshape().project(
    scale=200,
    translate=[320,70]
)

title: Geo Viz
text:
Pintado y bordes

alt.Chart(gdf_ne).mark_geoshape(
    fill='lightgray',
    stroke = 'white'
).project(
    scale=200,
    translate=[320,70]
)

title: Geo Viz
text:
Mapa de puntos

gdf_sel = gdf_ne.query("CONTINENT == 'South America'")

basemap = alt.Chart(gdf_sel).mark_geoshape(
    fill='lightgray',
    stroke = 'white'
)

bubbles = alt.Chart(gdf_sel).transform_calculate(
    centroid = alt.expr.geoCentroid(None, alt.datum)
).mark_circle().encode(
    longitude='centroid[0]:Q',
    latitude='centroid[1]:Q',
    size='POP_EST'
)

basemap + bubbles

title: Geo Viz
text:
Mapa Coroplético

alt.Chart(gdf_sel).mark_geoshape().encode(
    color='POP_EST',
    tooltip=[
        'POP_EST'
    ]
)

section: Merge de los 2 DS: geojson + df data.unemployment

title: Geo Viz
text:
Mapa de puntos

us_map = alt.Chart(states).mark_geoshape(
    stroke='white',
    fill='lightgray'
).project(
    type='albersUsa'
)


points = alt.Chart(state_pop).mark_circle().encode(
    longitude='longitude:Q',
    latitude='latitude:Q',

)

Derivar otra variable: asthma_cases_per_capita

state_pop['asthma_cases_per_capita'] = state_pop['number_of_asthma_cases']/state_pop['population']
state_pop

)

            maxbins=30

    as_=['Worldwide_Gross','density']

    alt.X('Worldwide_Gross'),
    alt.Y('density:Q', stack=None),
